join.title: We're hiring Members of our Technical Staff and Founder's Office
post.highground.body:
Introduction
This memo is an attempt to answer the question of where the "high ground" will be in software and computing outside of foundation model providers in the period 2026-2031. The backdrop being the proliferation of AI, both in terms of its persistent distribution into wider contexts of consumer and enterprise software, as well as its greater use in writing software. AI in Consumer and Enterprise Software
It is no great risk to predict that AI is going to continue to permeate software. This catalyzes four main changes compared to the status quo. 1. General systems growing at the expense of highly specialized ones. This is a function of the general understanding characteristic of all major LLMs. 2. The ability of software systems to be controlled by and made customizable through natural language. 3. The ability for software to become self-aware. Plugging code and user behavior into LLMs allows for an almost emergent capability, resulting in self-aware software. 4. The primacy of context in highly generalizable systems. Specialization is derived not from the predetermined architecture in the software, but rather from the general availability and thoughtful application of the most relevant user/business context at run/inference time. AI in Software Creation
It is also clear that AI is starting to dramatically change how software is written. We can similarly characterize four dimensions of this change: 1. The cost of software creation will go down. There will be more software. 2. Who writes software will change. Barriers to entry are falling as models with general capabilities are being harnessed by those without general capabilities but with specialized knowledge and taste. 3. Because these capabilities become generally available, software written by LLMs will become the median software, in other words, mediocre. The great firms that will win in their niche will have to be steered either by software architects with unique insights into how to engineer their systems efficiently and scalably, or by artisans and craftsmen who wed unique market insights and taste with the general capabilities of the LLMs. 4. AI is increasing the participation rate, both in terms of who's writing the software, but also in terms of how end users can adapt software to their unique preferences. The best systems therefore will be open, adaptable, allow for contributions from a broader developer community, and also provide high degrees of customization by their own end users. A note on Social Media
Even before the machines have landed in a big way on social media, it is apparent that in general, satisfaction with social media is low. The platforms are fragmented even while they are owned by an ever more concentrated set of hands. The algorithms drive their users towards content that generates an itch-scratch cycle of stimulation and craving. When you listen in on conversations at restaurants and bars, it is no exaggeration to say that often social media use is considered with disgust and self-loathing. It is not something we want to expose to our children, and it is not something that is enriching our lives or our relationships. Of course, there are a few pockets of communities for whom social media is still a net good. People who are bound together by a craft or by shared passions or interests. But these pockets of real utility are generally oases in the desert. Meaningful digital interactions between humans exist primarily within private spaces like messengers, chats, some forums, and email correspondence. The High Ground
The high ground will be at the intersection of humans interacting with each other and with preeminently capable, customizable, and personal systems which are delightful, simple to use, and retain the user's trust. Let's break each one of those aspects down. Interaction: As quantum physicists know, the universe emerges more from how objects interact rather than from what objects are. As machines gain the ability to understand natural human language, we will want to stream information that is informed through interactions rather than create and maintain static information structures. Capable: This means state of the art agentic capabilities paired with precision and the absence of major mistakes.
Customizable: This means that the end user (be they an individual, small business or company), can modify the system to the greatest possible degree according to their own preferences, and do so easily and intuitively. Personal: This goes beyond customizability even as it emerges partially from it. It means that a system incrementally feels like it belongs to the end user. It has adapted to the unique context of their lives and character. Delight: The system itself surprises the end user and brings them joy. There is a sense of wonder and light when one interacts with it. Simple: It is uncomplicated to begin with the software and to interact with it. Earning and Retaining Trust: How the product and the Company behind the product present themselves with credibility, engage with user context with transparency and refrain from abusing user trust or exposing the user to unacceptable security risks. Achieving that high ground through executing on the aspects above means unlocking an essential positive feedback loop between usage and context. Greater usage leads to deeper context, deeper context leads to more precise behavior by the system, more precise behavior by the system (particularly relative to peers or competitors) leads to greater usage. We believe that to the extent it retains the qualities above, the system should be maximally user-owned. Final Thoughts
We end with a new framing. Imagine a garden. Place yourself there in your mind's eye. You choose what to grow there. You tend to it with care. Your garden may be fenced to make it distinct from the gardens and spaces of others. It is a safe space, but it is not completely isolated from the world. Weather and climate and the outside world will confront you with constraints. Over time, your garden comes to reflect how you react to those constraints and decisions you have made over the years. Over time, your garden reflects you. In your garden lives an entity. The entity is rooted within your garden but is intelligent, worldly, and capable. This entity primarily exists to guide and help you, but can also interact with others. It knows deeply every inch of your garden, every budding flower, every fruit, even as it relates what is in your garden to the wide world outside. Like your garden, this entity comes to reflect you. You can send your entity forth from your garden, but even then it is colored and rooted in the choices you made in that dirt. At Earendil we are building the Entity and the Garden. Our system emerges from them both. We call it Lefos. Authored by Colin Daymond Hanna without the assistance of an LLM
post.highground.title: The High Ground
post.invitation.body: Hello, We believe in building in the open, sharing what we learn, what we craft, and where we're headed. If you're curious about software that strengthens human agency and bridges understanding, we'd love to have you along for the journey. [Leave us your email] to receive posts from us. Honest reflections on what we're building, why it matters, and what we're shaping together. Written by humans, not machines. We welcome your comments. We work out of Vienna, Austria, a city of coffee, culture, and good conversation. If you're passing through and want to say hello, drop us a line at rfc@earendil.com. The door is open. With care, Armin and Colin
post.invitation.title: An Invitation to Begin a Correspondence
purpose.agency.body: In a world hurtling towards AI, we believe humans are the best agents. AI is possibly the most powerful tool humanity has ever seen, but we wield the hammer, the hammer does not wield us.
purpose.agency.title: Strengthen human agency
purpose.bridge.body: Humanity is a community. We choose to look to our commonalities rather than our differences. We look to ground ourselves in truth and share truth with one another.
purpose.bridge.title: Bridge division and ignorance
purpose.charter: Our purpose is embedded in our founding charter:
purpose.craft.body: We believe in craft and we believe in open. Software shapes how information is understood and increasingly, how reality is perceived. That comes with responsibility.
purpose.craft.title: Craft software and open protocols
purpose.intro: Earendil is a public benefit corporation founded by Armin Ronacher and Colin Daymond Hanna
purpose.joy.body: Our products seek to bring people lasting joy and greater mutual understanding.
purpose.joy.title: Cultivate lasting joy and understanding
values.agents.1: 我们相信人类能动性。我们作为人类有明确的意图，并使用一切可用工具来实现这一意图
values.agents.2: 我们集体的神经多样性是我们的力量和护城河
values.agents.3: 最大化利用技术但不要懒惰。记住你是斧头。保持头脑敏锐，不要无知地成为他人意图的促进者
values.agents.title: 我们是行动者
values.ambitious.1: 我们不害怕极其雄心勃勃
values.ambitious.2: 但我们对制定可信计划一丝不苟
values.ambitious.3: 我们需要说服自己相信这条道路，并理解我们如何踏入天空
values.ambitious.title: 雄心勃勃的目标，可信的步骤
values.beauty.2: 只有你能将自己版本的美带到世界上
values.beauty.3: 精心制作某物需要努力和精通。我们庆祝两者
values.beauty.title: 创造美
values.charming.1: 礼貌和有魅力大有帮助，特别是在压力下
values.charming.2: 我们以带来轻松，甚至微笑的方式对待他人
values.charming.3: 我们倾向于高估他人对我们想法的理解程度。记住要过度沟通。
values.charming.title: 保持魅力
values.compassionate.1: 保持善良。我们首先是人，其次才是工作者。记住每个人都会有糟糕的日子
values.compassionate.2: 首先选择慷慨。世界不是零和游戏。我们可以共同创造丰富
values.compassionate.3: 假设恶意是自我实现的预言。假设善意会带来善意
values.compassionate.title: 保持同理心
values.curious.1: 我们保持好奇心和对新知识的渴望
values.curious.2: 我们足够谦逊地接受有很多我们不知道的事情，但也足够自信地通过自己的理性挑战既定智慧
values.curious.title: 原创好奇心
values.family.1: 我们的家庭可能是最亲密的朋友、父母、兄弟姐妹、配偶和孩子，或者我们的狗。这些是将定义我们生活的关系。它们排在第一位
values.family.2: 我们以强度和高标准追求工作，但我们拒绝为了一个而牺牲另一个。在冲突中，家庭优先。
values.family.title: 家庭第一
values.focused.1: 我们专注于自己能控制的事情。不让其他事情分散我们的注意力
values.focused.2: 对于我们能控制的事情，我们自豪地交付。我们利用专注来实现卓越
values.focused.3: 我们专注于卓越的结果，而不是卓越的流程
values.focused.title: 专注卓越
values.positive.1: 我们深切关心为积极结果做出贡献
values.positive.2: 我们可以看着父母、伴侣、朋友或孩子的眼睛，与他们分享我们所建造的目的和现实，包括任何负面外部性，并感到自豪
values.positive.3: 保持北极星向北。我们不妥协我们目标的清晰度
values.positive.title: 积极意图与结果
values.relentless.1: 随着我们力量和权力的增加，我们对行动和决策的考虑也应该增加。随着我们的成长和成功，我们变得更加深思熟虑，不那么冷酷
values.relentless.2: 我们体贴和深思熟虑，但当我们确定了最佳行动方案时，我们在追求这���目标时坚持不懈
values.relentless.title: 深思但坚持不懈
values.responsibility.1: 每个人都应该像公司所有者一样思考
values.responsibility.2: 每项任务都有明确的负责人，该负责人有明确的责任
values.responsibility.3: 我们寻求在认可自己之前先认可他人的贡献
values.responsibility.title: 彻底负责，慷慨认可
values.techMeans.1: 我们使用软件，因为这是我们所知道的
values.techMeans.2: 但我们不是来销售可爱的代码、有趣的算法或编程语言的
values.techMeans.3: 如果我们能用电子表格比用代码更好地经营这个业务，我们会这样做
values.techMeans.title: 技术是手段，不是目的
values.vulnerable.1: 脆弱孕育信任，隐瞒孕育距离
values.vulnerable.2: 减少假设和流言。有疑问时，直接与主要来源验证
values.vulnerable.3: 追求真理意味着承认错误。揭示错误应该被庆祝，而不是被批评
values.vulnerable.title: 透明脆弱
values.weNotI.1: 我们相信最好的团队大于各部分的总和
values.weNotI.2: 我们寻求最大化团队效用，但认识到彼此的优势和劣势
values.weNotI.3: 我们个人保持谦逊。作为团队我们展现自信
values.weNotI.title: 我们而非我
